---
title: "Southern Beaksedge (Rhynchospora microcarpa)"
slug: "rhynchospora-microcarpa"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474883374/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Southern Beaksedge"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/474883374/medium.jpg" alt="Southern Beaksedge" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/mj1387">
mj1387</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264452554">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=167883)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Rare species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and savannas.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like wetland plant characterized by its slender stems and small clusters of spikelets with relatively small fruits. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and savannas, often in sandy soils. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to vegetation structure in wetland communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/167883-Rhynchospora-microcarpa)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/3007)
